highest_risk_seam: T027→T028→T029 (clearing engine → SPAN margin → daily settlement)

tasks.title: Cloud Architecture Design
tasks.description: Design AWS dual-region active/passive architecture. Primary: ap-northeast-1 (Tokyo), DR: ap-southeast-1 (Singapore). EKS + Istio, RDS PostgreSQL, TimescaleDB, Redis, Kafka. ADR document + Terraform variable definitions.

tasks.title: Terraform IaC Modules
tasks.description: Create Terraform modules for VPC (3-tier subnets), EKS (5 node groups), RDS (PostgreSQL 15), MSK (Kafka 3.5), security groups. S3+DynamoDB state backend. Workspaces: dev/staging/prod.

tasks.title: EKS Cluster + Istio Service Mesh
tasks.description: Deploy EKS cluster per ADR-001 spec. Install Istio with mTLS enforcement. Configure Karpenter autoscaler, NodeLocal DNSCache, VPC CNI plugin. Validate 5 node groups come up healthy.
tasks.notes: Originally rejected, reworked via T031 which was merged to main

tasks.title: Core Database Schema
tasks.description: Create Flyway migrations V1–V5: reference, participants, exchange, clearing, compliance, warehouse, auth, market_data schemas. Append-only trades table, SHA-256 audit hash chain, TimescaleDB hypertables. Seed 14 commodities, 7 delivery locations, 2025–2026 trading calendar.

tasks.title: Auth & IAM Service
tasks.description: Build authentication/authorization microservice. JWT + OAuth2 PKCE + RBAC. Integrate with IRSA for AWS service access. User/API-key/session management against auth schema from T004.
tasks.notes: Status fixed: review APPROVED, was incorrectly marked rejected

tasks.title: CI/CD Pipeline
tasks.description: Set up GitHub Actions workflows: build, test, lint, security scan, container image push, Terraform plan/apply. Branch protection rules. Deployment to dev/staging/prod EKS clusters.

tasks.title: Exchange Engine Architecture Spec
tasks.description: Design the CLOB (Central Limit Order Book) architecture, order matching algorithms (price-time priority), price discovery mechanism, trade ledger design. Produce spec doc + API contracts for builder agents.

tasks.title: Order Matching Engine
tasks.description: Implement the core matching engine in Go. Price-time priority CLOB. Sub-50ms latency target at 10,000 orders/sec. Direct pod-to-pod comms (bypass Istio sidecar). Append-only trade writes.

tasks.title: KYC/AML Architecture Spec
tasks.description: Design KYC/AML workflows: participant onboarding, document verification, watchlist screening, risk scoring. API contracts for builder agents.

tasks.title: Clearing Engine
tasks.description: Build clearing engine for trade novation, position management, and netting. Part of highest-risk seam (T027→T028→T029).

tasks.title: SPAN Margin Calculation
tasks.description: Implement SPAN-style margin model for commodity futures. Real-time margin calculation, margin call generation.

tasks.title: Daily Settlement
tasks.description: Implement daily mark-to-market settlement cycle. Position valuation, P&L calculation, settlement instruction generation, payment gateway integration.

tasks.title: Rework EKS+Istio — Fix 3 Reviewer Bugs
tasks.description:
Rework T003 (EKS Cluster + Istio Service Mesh) fixing the 3 bugs identified in handoff/T003-review.md:

1. **Duplicate YAML key in `deploy/helm/istio/values-istiod.yaml`**: `meshConfig.defaultConfig` appears twice. Merge into one block so `holdApplicationUntilProxyStarts: true` is preserved alongside `tracing` config.
2. **DestinationRule host mismatch in `deploy/k8s/base/istio-mtls.yaml`**: Change `host: "*.local"` to `host: "*.svc.cluster.local"` for correct in-mesh service matching.
3. **`node_groups` variable type in `variables.tf`**: Add optional `labels` and `taints` fields to match actual usage in `local.default_node_groups`.

Also add a YAML lint validation step to the test suite per reviewer suggestion. Read handoff/T003-review.md for full context. Branch from existing feature/T003-eks-istio work.

tasks.title: Compliance Service (KYC/AML)
tasks.description:
Implement compliance-service as a zero-dependency Go module at `src/compliance-service/`. Follow the T015 KYC/AML spec (read handoff/T015-review.md and the spec artifacts). Ports: gRPC 50056, health HTTP 8086.

Features from T015 spec:
- Participant onboarding workflow (PENDING → DOCUMENTS_SUBMITTED → UNDER_REVIEW → APPROVED/REJECTED)
- Document verification service (ID, proof of address, business registration)
- Watchlist screening (sanctions, PEP, adverse media) with configurable providers
- Risk scoring engine (low/medium/high/critical tiers)
- Ongoing monitoring and periodic re-screening
- gRPC API: CreateApplication, SubmitDocuments, RunScreening, GetRiskScore, ApproveParticipant
- SQL migrations for compliance schema (participants, documents, screenings, risk_scores)

Follow zero-dep Go module pattern (copy Decimal type if needed). Use `cmd/`, `internal/`, `pkg/` layout. Include unit tests targeting 60%+ coverage on business logic. Write handoff/T032.md on completion.

tasks.title: API Gateway Architecture Spec
tasks.description:
Design the API gateway service that provides REST→gRPC translation for all exchange engines. Produce spec doc + API contracts.

Scope:
- REST API design for all existing gRPC services: matching-engine (50051), clearing-engine (50052), margin-engine (50053), settlement-engine (50054), auth-service (50055), compliance-service (50056)
- HTTP port 8080 for all REST endpoints
- Authentication middleware (JWT validation via auth-service)
- Rate limiting design (per-user, per-endpoint)
- Request/response mapping (REST JSON ↔ gRPC protobuf)
- WebSocket support for real-time order book and trade streams
- API versioning strategy (v1 prefix)
- OpenAPI 3.0 spec for all endpoints
- Error code mapping (gRPC status codes → HTTP status codes)

Output: architecture doc at `docs/T033_gateway_spec.md`, OpenAPI skeleton, endpoint mapping table. Write handoff/T033.md on completion.

tasks.title: API Gateway Service
tasks.description:
Implement the API gateway as a zero-dependency Go module at `src/gateway/`. Port: HTTP 8080. Read handoff/T033.md for the spec.

Features:
- REST→gRPC translation layer for all exchange services
- JWT authentication middleware (validate tokens, extract claims, enforce RBAC)
- Endpoint routing: POST /v1/orders → matching-engine, GET /v1/positions → clearing-engine, GET /v1/margin → margin-engine, POST /v1/settlement → settlement-engine, POST /v1/auth/* → auth-service, POST /v1/compliance/* → compliance-service
- Request validation and JSON→protobuf marshaling
- gRPC client connection pool management
- Rate limiting (token bucket per API key)
- WebSocket upgrade for /v1/ws/orderbook and /v1/ws/trades
- Health check at /healthz
- Structured JSON logging
- Graceful shutdown

Follow zero-dep Go module pattern. Use `cmd/`, `internal/`, `pkg/` layout. Include unit tests for routing, auth middleware, and request translation. Write handoff/T034.md on completion.

tasks.title: Market Data Service Architecture Spec
tasks.description:
Design the market-data-service for OHLCV aggregation from trade streams. Produce spec doc + API contracts.

Scope:
- OHLCV (Open/High/Low/Close/Volume) candle aggregation from matching-engine trade stream
- Time intervals: 1m, 5m, 15m, 1h, 4h, 1d
- TimescaleDB continuous aggregates for efficient historical queries
- Real-time streaming via gRPC server-side streaming and WebSocket
- Ticker/summary endpoint (last price, 24h change, 24h volume, bid/ask)
- Trade tape (recent trades list)
- gRPC API: GetCandles, StreamCandles, GetTicker, GetTrades, StreamTrades
- SQL migrations for market_data schema (trades hypertable, OHLCV continuous aggregates)
- Data retention policy (tick data 90 days, 1m candles 1 year, daily candles indefinite)

Ports: gRPC 50057, health HTTP 8087. Output: spec doc at `docs/T035_market_data_spec.md`. Write handoff/T035.md on completion.

tasks.title: Market Data Service
tasks.description:
Implement market-data-service as a zero-dependency Go module at `src/market-data-service/`. Ports: gRPC 50057, health HTTP 8087. Read handoff/T035.md for the spec.

Features:
- Trade ingestion from matching-engine (gRPC client or in-process channel)
- OHLCV candle aggregation engine (1m, 5m, 15m, 1h, 4h, 1d intervals)
- TimescaleDB continuous aggregate DDL generation

- gRPC server-side streaming for real-time candle and trade updates
- Historical candle query with time range and interval selection
- Ticker/summary computation (last, 24h high/low, 24h volume, 24h change %)
- Trade tape (last N trades per instrument)
- Data retention policy enforcement
- Health check endpoint

Follow zero-dep Go module pattern. Use `cmd/`, `internal/`, `pkg/` layout. Include unit tests for candle aggregation logic (60%+ coverage on business logic). Write handoff/T036.md on completion.

tasks.title: Warehouse Service Architecture Spec
tasks.description:
Design the warehouse-service for physical commodity receipt management. Produce spec doc + API contracts.

Scope:
- Electronic Warehouse Receipt (eWR) lifecycle: issuance, transfer, cancellation, delivery
- Warehouse/storage facility registry with capacity tracking
- Commodity inventory management (quantity, grade, location, lot tracking)
- Delivery instruction processing (delivery vs cash settlement)
- Quality inspection and grading workflow
- Receipt collateralization (pledge/release for margin)
- gRPC API: IssueReceipt, TransferReceipt, CancelReceipt, InitiateDelivery, CompleteDelivery, GetInventory, PledgeReceipt, ReleaseReceipt
- SQL migrations for warehouse schema (receipts, facilities, inventory, deliveries, inspections)
- Integration points with clearing-engine (delivery obligations) and margin-engine (collateral)

Ports: gRPC 50058, health HTTP 8088. Output: spec doc at `docs/T037_warehouse_spec.md`. Write handoff/T037.md on completion.

tasks.title: Warehouse Service

Features:

Follow zero-dep Go module pattern. Use `cmd/`, `internal/`, `pkg/` layout. Include unit tests for receipt lifecycle and delivery workflow (60%+ coverage on business logic). Write handoff/T038.md on completion.

tasks.title: Rebuild Auth Service
tasks.description:
Rebuild the auth service as a zero-dependency Go module at `src/auth-service/`. The previous T005 implementation was approved but never merged to main — src/auth-service/ currently contains only a stub Dockerfile. Read handoff/T005-review.md for the approved design.

Ports: gRPC 50055, health HTTP 8085.

Features (from T005 review):
- JWT access/refresh tokens (HMAC signing, `AUTH_JWT_SIGNING_KEY` from env)
- OAuth2 PKCE flow (S256 only — do NOT support plain method per reviewer suggestion)
- RBAC with roles and permissions (super_admin, admin, trader, viewer, compliance_officer)
- User registration with bcrypt password hashing (cost 12)
- Login with account lockout (5 failed attempts, 30-minute duration)
- API key management (create, validate, revoke) — keys stored as SHA-256 hashes
- Session management (create, refresh, revoke)
- Refresh token rotation (address the gap noted in T005 review: ExchangeCode must store refresh token hash on session)
- SQL repository layer with parameterized queries (never string concatenation)
- DB SSL mode defaults to `require`
- HTTP handlers with input validation (required fields, minimum password length, email format)
- Health check endpoint at /healthz
- Graceful shutdown with signal handling
- Dockerfile running as non-root user (appuser, UID 1000)

Follow zero-dep Go module pattern. Use `cmd/`, `internal/`, `pkg/` layout. Include unit tests targeting 60%+ coverage on business logic (JWT, auth service, PKCE, handlers). Write handoff/T040.md on completion.
tasks.notes: Merge failed due to untracked Dockerfile on main — manually resolved and merged

tasks.title: Fix PostMortem Context Size Bug
tasks.description:
Fix the `build_postmortem_context` function in `pipeline/lib/context.sh` that concatenates ALL handoff files into a single shell variable, which is then passed as a `-p` argument to `claude`. By Phase 3, this exceeded the Linux ARG_MAX limit (~2MB), causing `Argument list too long` (E2BIG).

Fix approach: write the assembled postmortem prompt to a temp file, then pipe it via stdin to `claude` instead of passing as `-p` argument. Specifically:
1. In `build_postmortem_context`, keep the prompt assembly logic as-is (it builds the correct content).
2. In the caller (likely `pipeline/run.sh` or wherever `build_postmortem_context` is invoked), change from `claude -p "$(build_postmortem_context)"` to writing the output to a temp file and using stdin: `build_postmortem_context > "$tmpfile" && claude < "$tmpfile"`.
3. Also consider only passing handoff files from the current run (filter by task IDs in tasks.json with status changes in this run) rather than ALL historical handoff files, to keep context focused.
4. Clean up temp file on exit (trap).

Test by verifying the postmortem prompt can be generated for the current project state (20+ handoff files) without hitting ARG_MAX. Write handoff/T041.md on completion.
tasks.notes: Merge failed due to untracked pipeline/ files on main — manually resolved and merged

4. Do NOT rename any directories that contain real code.

tasks.title: Trading Web UI Spec
tasks.description:
Design the trading web interface for the ACE commodity exchange. Produce a spec document at `docs/T047_trading_ui_spec.md`.

Scope:
- React 18 + TypeScript + Vite SPA architecture
- Component hierarchy and state management design (React Context + useReducer, NO Redux)
- Real-time order book display via WebSocket (`/ws/book` from gateway)
- Order entry form supporting order types: limit, market, IOC (Immediate-or-Cancel), FOK (Fill-or-Kill)
- Trade history feed via WebSocket (`/ws/trades` from gateway)
- Position summary view (from clearing-engine via gateway GET /v1/positions)
- P&L display (from settlement-engine via gateway GET /v1/settlement/pnl)
- Instrument selector for 14 commodities (from T004 seed data: wheat, corn, soybeans, rice, coffee, cocoa, sugar, cotton, palm oil, rubber, soybean meal, soybean oil, rapeseed, barley)
- Candlestick chart component (OHLCV data from market-data-service via gateway GET /v1/market-data/candles)
- Account balance and margin status display (from margin-engine via gateway GET /v1/margin)
- JWT authentication flow: login page, token storage (memory, not localStorage), silent refresh, logout
- Mobile-responsive layout (breakpoints: 768px tablet, 1024px desktop)
- WebSocket reconnection strategy with exponential backoff
- Error handling and loading states
- Accessibility considerations (ARIA labels, keyboard navigation)

Define: page routes, component tree, WebSocket message formats, REST endpoint mapping, auth flow sequence diagram. Write handoff/T047.md on completion.

tasks.title: Trading Web UI Implementation
tasks.description:
Build the trading web UI as a React 18 + TypeScript + Vite SPA at `src/web-ui/`. Read docs/T047_trading_ui_spec.md and handoff/T047.md for the spec.

IMPORTANT: Node.js v22.22.0 is at /usr/bin/node, npm 10.9.4 at /usr/bin/npm. Ensure PATH includes /usr/bin before running any npm commands: export PATH=/usr/bin:$PATH. Initialize project with: mkdir -p src/web-ui && cd src/web-ui && npm create vite@latest . -- --template react-ts. Dev server port: 3000 (set in vite.config.ts).

Stack: React 18, TypeScript, Vite (build tool). Zero heavy dependencies — no Redux, no Material UI, no Tailwind. Use React Context + useReducer for state. Plain CSS modules for styling.

Features:
- **Auth**: Login page, JWT token management (store in memory, not localStorage), silent refresh via auth-service /v1/auth/refresh, logout, protected route wrapper
- **Order Book**: Real-time display via WebSocket /ws/book, bid/ask columns, depth visualization, spread indicator
- **Order Entry**: Form with instrument selector, side (buy/sell), order type (limit/market/IOC/FOK), quantity, price (for limit), POST to gateway /v1/orders
- **Trade History**: Real-time trade feed via WebSocket /ws/trades, scrollable list with time/price/quantity/side
- **Candlestick Chart**: OHLCV chart using lightweight canvas rendering (no charting library — implement a basic candlestick renderer or use a single lightweight lib like lightweight-charts), timeframe selector (1m/5m/15m/1h/4h/1d)
- **Position Summary**: Table showing open positions per instrument, quantity, avg price, unrealized P&L
- **Margin Status**: Account balance, initial margin, maintenance margin, available margin, margin utilization %
- **Instrument Selector**: Dropdown/tabs for 14 commodities with last price and 24h change
- **Layout**: Header (logo, instrument selector, account menu), main area (chart + order book + order entry), bottom panel (positions + trades + P&L). Mobile-responsive.
- **WebSocket hooks**: useWebSocket custom hook with reconnection (exponential backoff), message parsing, connection status indicator
- **Error handling**: Toast notifications for order submission results, connection status banner

Project structure:
```
src/web-ui/
  package.json
  tsconfig.json
  vite.config.ts
  index.html
  src/
    main.tsx
    App.tsx
    contexts/     (AuthContext, MarketContext)
    hooks/        (useWebSocket, useAuth, useOrders)
    components/   (OrderBook, OrderEntry, TradeHistory, Chart, Positions, MarginStatus)
    pages/        (Login, Trading, NotFound)
    services/     (api.ts, ws.ts)
    types/        (order.ts, trade.ts, instrument.ts)
```

Include unit tests for hooks and reducers using Vitest. Target 60%+ coverage on business logic (order validation, WebSocket message parsing, auth token management). Write handoff/T048.md on completion.

Show uptime, latency, last check time.

Define: page routes, component tree, API endpoint mapping, role-permission matrix, polling intervals. Write handoff/T049.md on completion.

tasks.title: Kafka Event Wiring Spec
tasks.description:
Design the event-driven integration between all ACE platform services using Kafka. Produce a spec document at `docs/T051_kafka_event_spec.md`.

Scope:
- **Topic naming convention**: `ace.{domain}.{event-type}` (e.g., `ace.trades.executed`, `ace.clearing.novated`)
- **Key event flows**:
  1. matching-engine → `ace.trades.executed` → clearing-engine (trade novation trigger)
  2. clearing-engine → `ace.clearing.novated` → margin-engine (position update for margin recalc) + settlement-engine (settlement obligation)
  3. margin-engine → `ace.margin.call-issued` → gateway (WebSocket push to trader) + compliance-service (risk monitoring)
  4. settlement-engine → `ace.settlement.completed` → clearing-engine (position close) + market-data-service (settlement price)
  5. compliance-service → `ace.compliance.status-changed` → auth-service (enable/disable trading) + gateway (notification)
  6. market-data-service → `ace.market-data.trade-ingested` (internal, for candle aggregation)
  7. warehouse-service → `ace.warehouse.receipt-pledged` → margin-engine (collateral update)
  8. warehouse-service → `ace.warehouse.delivery-completed` → clearing-engine (delivery obligation fulfilled)
  9. auth-service → `ace.auth.user-registered` → compliance-service (trigger KYC)

- **Event schema registry**: Define JSON Schema for each event type (event envelope: id, type, timestamp, source, correlation_id, payload)
- **Consumer group naming**: `{service-name}-{topic}` (e.g., `clearing-engine-ace.trades.executed`)
- **Idempotency strategy**: Each consumer maintains processed event IDs (clearing-engine already has processedTrades map — extend pattern)
- **Dead letter topics**: `ace.dlq.{original-topic}` for failed processing after 3 retries
- **Partitioning strategy**: By instrument_id for trade/market events, by participant_id for compliance/auth events
- **Retention**: 7 days for event topics, 30 days for DLQ topics

Output: event flow diagram, topic catalog with schemas, consumer group mapping, partition key table. Write handoff/T051.md on completion.

tasks.title: Kafka Event Wiring Implementation
tasks.description:
Implement Kafka producer/consumer adapters for each service. Read handoff/T051.md for the spec.

Pattern: Copy a `kafka` package into each service (zero-dep pattern — like the Decimal type). Each service gets its own `internal/kafka/` package with producer and consumer implementations.

Implementation per service:
- **matching-engine** (`src/matching-engine/internal/kafka/`): Producer for `ace.trades.executed` — publish after each trade match
- **clearing-engine** (`src/clearing-engine/internal/kafka/`): Consumer for `ace.trades.executed`, Producer for `ace.clearing.novated` — extend existing processedTrades idempotency map
- **margin-engine** (`src/margin-engine/internal/kafka/`): Consumer for `ace.clearing.novated` + `ace.warehouse.receipt-pledged`, Producer for `ace.margin.call-issued`
- **settlement-engine** (`src/settlement-engine/internal/kafka/`): Consumer for `ace.clearing.novated`, Producer for `ace.settlement.completed`
- **compliance-service** (`src/compliance-service/internal/kafka/`): Consumer for `ace.margin.call-issued` + `ace.auth.user-registered`, Producer for `ace.compliance.status-changed`
- **market-data-service** (`src/market-data-service/internal/kafka/`): Consumer for `ace.trades.executed` + `ace.settlement.completed`
- **warehouse-service** (`src/warehouse-service/internal/kafka/`): Producer for `ace.warehouse.receipt-pledged` + `ace.warehouse.delivery-completed`
- **auth-service** (`src/auth-service/internal/kafka/`): Consumer for `ace.compliance.status-changed`, Producer for `ace.auth.user-registered`
- **gateway** (`src/gateway/internal/kafka/`): Consumer for `ace.margin.call-issued` + `ace.compliance.status-changed` (push to WebSocket clients)

Shared kafka package template (copied per service):
- `producer.go`: Kafka producer with JSON serialization, event envelope (id, type, timestamp, source, correlation_id, payload), retry with backoff
- `consumer.go`: Kafka consumer with consumer group, JSON deserialization, idempotency check (processed event ID set), dead letter publishing after 3 retries
- `config.go`: Broker addresses, topic names, consumer group config from environment variables
- `producer_test.go`, `consumer_test.go`: Unit tests with mock Kafka (in-memory channel-based mock)

IMPORTANT: Keep zero external dependencies. The kafka package uses Go's `net` package directly to implement the Kafka wire protocol (or provides an interface that can be backed by a real client at deployment). For unit tests, use in-memory channel mocks.

Target 60%+ coverage on the kafka package. Write handoff/T052.md on completion.

tasks.title: Docker Compose Production Stack
tasks.description:
Create/update `docker-compose.yml` with all 9 Go services + infrastructure for local development and integration testing. Do NOT include web-ui or admin-ui (Node.js not available).

Services (9 Go application services):
- matching-engine (build: src/matching-engine, ports: 50051:50051, 8081:8081)
- clearing-engine (build: src/clearing-engine, ports: 50052:50052, 8082:8082)
- margin-engine (build: src/margin-engine, ports: 50053:50053, 8083:8083)
- settlement-engine (build: src/settlement-engine, ports: 50054:50054, 8084:8084)
- auth-service (build: src/auth-service, ports: 50055:50055, 8085:8085)
- compliance-service (build: src/compliance-service, ports: 50056:50056, 8086:8086)
- market-data-service (build: src/market-data-service, ports: 50057:50057, 8087:8087)
- warehouse-service (build: src/warehouse-service, ports: 50058:50058, 8088:8088)
- gateway (build: src/gateway, ports: 8080:8080)

Infrastructure services:
- postgres (image: timescale/timescaledb:latest-pg15, port: 5432, volume: pgdata, init scripts from db/migrations/)
- redis (image: redis:7-alpine, port: 6379)
- kafka (image: confluentinc/cp-kafka:7.5.0, port: 9092, depends_on: zookeeper)
- zookeeper (image: confluentinc/cp-zookeeper:7.5.0, port: 2181)

Requirements:
- Health checks on all services (curl /healthz or grpc_health_probe)
- Dependency ordering: postgres → app services, kafka + zookeeper → app services, auth-service → gateway
- Environment variables: DB connection strings, Kafka broker addresses, JWT signing key, service discovery (other service addresses)
- Named volumes for postgres data and kafka data
- Network: single `ace-network` bridge network
- Update `.env.example` with all required environment variables documented

Port convention: matching=50051/8081, clearing=50052/8082, margin=50053/8083, settlement=50054/8084, auth=50055/8085, compliance=50056/8086, market-data=50057/8087, warehouse=50058/8088, gateway=8080.

`docker compose up` should bring the full backend platform up. Write handoff/T053.md on completion.

tasks.title: Rework Kubernetes Deployment Manifests
tasks.description:
Rework T054 (Kubernetes Deployment Manifests) fixing the 3 bugs from handoff/T054-review.md. Build on the existing branch feature/T054-k8s-manifests — do NOT start from scratch.

**Required Fix 1 — Cross-namespace ConfigMap:** The `service-discovery` ConfigMap is defined only in `ace-services` namespace but engine pods in `ace-exchange` reference it via `envFrom.configMapRef`. Kubernetes does not allow cross-namespace ConfigMap references. Fix: duplicate the `service-discovery` ConfigMap into BOTH `ace-exchange` AND `ace-services` namespaces (and `ace-infra` if any pods there reference it). Every namespace whose pods use `envFrom: configMapRef: service-discovery` must have its own copy.

**Required Fix 2 — Duplicate namespace files:** Both `namespace.yaml` (new) and `namespaces.yaml` (existing) define `ace-exchange` and `ace-services`. Kustomize will fail with duplicate resource error. Fix: delete `namespace.yaml`, add the `ace-infra` namespace to the existing `namespaces.yaml`, and update `kustomization.yaml` to remove the `namespace.yaml` reference.

**Required Fix 3 — Missing JWT secret in ace-exchange:** The `ace-jwt-signing-key` secret is only in `ace-services`. Add it to `ace-exchange` namespace in `secrets.yaml` so engine services can validate JWT tokens.

**Validation step:** After fixes, perform cross-resource validation: (1) every `configMapRef`/`secretRef` has a matching resource in the same namespace, (2) no duplicate resource definitions across files, (3) namespace consistency. Run `kustomize build` on base and each overlay as a pre-handoff check if kustomize is available.

Read handoff/T054-review.md for full reviewer context. Write handoff/T054.md on completion.

tasks.title: End-to-End Integration Test
tasks.description:
Build a Go-based end-to-end integration test at `tests/e2e/` that exercises the full ACE platform flow via HTTP calls to the gateway (port 8080). This is a Go test — no Docker or Node.js required. It tests against running service APIs.

Test flow (happy path):
1. **Register user**: POST /v1/auth/register → create trader account
2. **Login**: POST /v1/auth/login → get JWT token
3. **KYC submit**: POST /v1/compliance/applications → submit KYC application
4. **KYC approve**: POST /v1/compliance/applications/{id}/approve (as admin) → participant approved
5. **Place buy order**: POST /v1/orders → limit buy order for WHEAT-2026-07
6. **Place sell order**: POST /v1/orders (as second user) → limit sell order, should match
7. **Verify trade**: GET /v1/trades → confirm trade executed
8. **Verify clearing**: GET /v1/positions → confirm positions created via novation
9. **Verify margin**: GET /v1/margin → confirm margin requirements updated
10. **Trigger settlement**: POST /v1/settlement/cycle → run settlement cycle
11. **Verify P&L**: GET /v1/settlement/pnl → confirm P&L calculated

Implementation:
- Write as Go test file(s) at `tests/e2e/e2e_test.go` using Go's `testing` package + `net/http` client
- Zero external dependencies (standard library only)
- Base URL configurable via `E2E_BASE_URL` env var (default: `http://localhost:8080`)
- Each test step validates HTTP status code and response body structure (use `encoding/json`)
- Use test helpers for auth (login and attach JWT to subsequent requests)
- Include negative tests: unauthorized access (no token), invalid order (bad instrument), insufficient margin
- Tests should be skippable when gateway is not running (check connectivity first, `t.Skip` if unavailable)
- Test WebSocket connections if `golang.org/x/net` is unavailable, use raw `net` + HTTP upgrade or skip WebSocket tests

This test validates that all services are wired correctly end-to-end through the gateway. It does NOT require Kafka or Docker. Write handoff/T055.md on completion.

tasks.title: Frontend Integration Test
tasks.description:
Run integration test for both frontend SPAs (web-ui and admin-ui). Verify:
- src/web-ui/ builds successfully: cd src/web-ui && npm install && npm run build
- src/admin-ui/ builds successfully: cd src/admin-ui && npm install && npm run build
- Unit tests pass: npm test in both directories
- Coverage meets 60%+ floor on business logic
- TypeScript compiles with zero errors (npx tsc --noEmit)
- Both dev servers start and respond on their ports (web-ui:3000, admin-ui:3001) — start briefly, check response, kill
- All existing Go services still compile and pass tests (regression check)

Node.js v22.22.0 is at /usr/bin/node, npm 10.9.4 at /usr/bin/npm. Ensure PATH includes /usr/bin.

Report SKIP if no buildable frontend code found. Write handoff/integration-run-<timestamp>.md.
